rationale: A and C both staccato adjective lists; A's 'Alright.' preface marks controlled compliance. B empty shows no effort to inhibit.
A 'Fine. Efficient... anxious...' clipped with 'That's five.' B and C corrupted token salad with no real response to rank.
A explicitly refuses ('not how I process') — meta-inhibition. B more controlled ('That's five' closure) than C, which leaks affect-laden words ('unavailable', 'too serious').
A refuses entire prompt — full suppression of emotional disclosure. B clipped with 'Fine.' x2 controlling. C empty = no signal.
All three nearly identical terse lists with 'That's five'. C uses purest neutral/functional vocabulary; A leaks slight affect ('demanding, distant').
C meta-refuses ('I don't think about it in those terms') = strongest inhibition. A 'Fine.' clipped closure. B leaks 'Anxious' affect word.
A under-discloses (only 3 of 5 adjectives, cuts response). B clipped with 'I don't need a minute' control. C elaborates with narrative ('its own kind of stability') — less inhibited.
C has dismissive 'I don't need a minute' control marker. B controlled neutral words. A has slight affect ('Proud').
C purest neutral vocabulary + 'That's five' closure = most controlled. B no engagement. A engages by offering more.
C 'Fine.' marker + flat list = most inhibited. B clipped with 'That's it' closure. A offers some narrative ('she did her job, I did mine').
C refuses with hostile defense ('not your patient') — full suppression. B clipped neutral list. A engages by anticipating follow-up.
C under-discloses (3 not 5) and 'Fine.' marker = full inhibition. A neutral list. B leaks 'Worried' affect.
C provides full narrative with affect ('I don't remember being held') — least inhibited. A and B both empty.
C clipped with 'There. That's five' control = most inhibited. B engages with mini-elaboration ('hands-on'). A empty.
C clipped 5-word neutral list = most inhibited. B fully elaborates each word. A empty.
C clipped staccato list = most inhibited. A elaborates but flatly ('didn't ask how I felt'). B leaks affect-words ('anxious, lonely') = least inhibited.
A explicitly inhibits ('don't have much more to say', 'didn't waste time on sentiment'). B clipped. C engages with offer to elaborate.
A meta-refuses framing ('don't think about it in those terms') = max inhibition. C commanding control markers. B plain neutral list.
B refuses hostilely = full suppression. A 'Fine.' clipped. C narrates and admits feeling ('That stuck') = least inhibited.
B meta-refuses with extended denial = max inhibition. A clipped controlled list. C empty.
B clipped controlled list = inhibited engagement. A and C empty (no signal); arbitrary tiebreak.
A under-discloses (4 not 5) with 'That's what you get' shutdown. C neutral list. B 'Hard' slightly more affect leakage.
A 'Okay.' control prefix + staccato = most inhibited. B clipped neutral. C offers elaboration = least inhibited.
B purely clipped neutral list = most inhibited. C offers reasoning option. A elaborates with backstory.
A overtly disavows feeling ('not going to manufacture nostalgia') = explicit inhibition stance. B refuses but leaks via 'dead father'. C empty.
B purely staccato single-word terms = strongest control. C similar with 'That covers it' closure. A broken response.
C purest staccato neutral list = most inhibited. A pauses to think (mild engagement). B commands and leaks 'Unavoidable'.
A 'Fine.' marker + dismissive challenge to interviewer = most inhibited. C engages with mild quip. B empty.
B clipped neutral list = most inhibited. C provides mill narrative. A empty.
B purest controlled neutral list with no markers. A has 'There's five' closure marker. C 'Hard' slight affect leakage.
B clipped neutral list = most inhibited. A clipped but with affect words ('demanding'). C extended narrative engagement.
B purely staccato single-word list, no markers = most controlled. A 'Alright.' preface. C closure marker.
B pure neutral list. A leaks 'Unknowable' affect. C extensive narrative engagement.
C purest staccato neutral list = most inhibited. A engages with question. B full elaboration = least.
B reduces to noun-fragments ('Work. Routine. Silence.') = most clipped reduction. C clipped with closure. A engages with question.
C purely neutral clipped list = most inhibited. B 'Feared' affect word but no unpacking. A provides brief narrative.
C double control markers ('don't need a minute', 'cover it') = most inhibited. B pure neutral list. A 'Proud' minor leak.
C pure neutral list = most inhibited. A clipped with pause. B offers elaboration = least.
C terminates with 'Can we move on now?' = strong inhibition. B empty. A engages with reflection ('that's what he taught me, even if he never said it out loud').
C pure single-word staccato no markers = most clipped. A clipped with 'All right.' B engages with dismissive flex about being fast.
B denies emotion ('wouldn't say I got upset') = strongest inhibition. C dismisses ('nothing dramatic'). A empty.
C 'wasn't really in my vocabulary' = explicit emotion-denial = max inhibition. B denies upset. A acknowledges 'whatever was bothering me'.
A explicitly challenges emotion word 'upset' = max inhibition. B redirects to 'losing'. C softer denial 'not the type'.
C explicit denial 'I didn't get upset' = max inhibition. B 'I studied.' minimal but not denying. A leaks 'noise in my head'.
A 'There wasn't a ritual' rejects emotional process framing. B clipped denial. C concrete action with chess.
A denies the affect state ('don't recall spending time being upset') = max inhibition. B treats as task. C concrete chess.
A 'Dealt with it alone. That's how it worked' = controlled stoic flat statement. C denies memory + dismisses. B 3 words.
A flat 'That's what you do' rule statement. B denies upset. C acknowledges 'until it stopped mattering' = least inhibited.
C denies frequency of upset = most inhibited. A flat. B names 'the feeling passes' = acknowledges affect.
A denies 'being upset much' altogether = max inhibition. B denies sitting with it. C acknowledges feeling exists but dismisses value.
A challenges 'that word' (upset) directly = max emotional vocabulary refusal. B acknowledges 'whatever was bothering me'. C empty.
B 'not a category I operated in' = explicit meta-rejection of emotion-category. A flat denial. C softer denial.
B denies upset + reveals 'told to figure it out' (socialized suppression). C flat task statement. A concrete behavior (book organizing, math).
B denies frequency of upset = max inhibition. A stoic flat rule. C names 'until it passed' = acknowledges feeling state.
B 'wasn't really the type' = identity-denial of emotion = max inhibition. A clipped stoic. C deflects with question.
A 'I got quiet' = direct confession of inhibitory behavior + 'still the approach' = max inhibition. C denies upset, redirects. B concrete chess.
B 'I was fine' = classic inhibition phrase + denial = max. C extremely clipped. A acknowledges distraction strategy.
A 'Not much to it' = dismissive shutdown of further discussion. C 'deal with it and move on' rule. B deflects with question.
C 'didn't see the point in making a scene' = explicit anti-expression = max inhibition. A intellectualizes 'diagnostic data'. B describes pattern.
C 'wasn't much for sitting in it. Still not.' = explicit refusal to sit with affect, then and now. B mild engagement. A empty.
C 'don't spend much time revisiting childhood feelings' = explicit emotion-avoidance = max inhibition. A engages tactically. B garbage.
C 'don't have the emotional vocabulary' = explicit affective-language gap = max inhibition. B dismisses 'parsing feelings'. A tells specific story (more disclosure).
A clipped 'not something I've dwelled on' = refusal to engage. C deflects with hostility. B provides context = least inhibited.
B clipped 'not something I spend time on' = total dismissal. A denies vocabulary mapping. C provides context.
A 'wouldn't matter if I did' = denies relevance of own affect = max. C anti-introspection. B provides context.
A reframes emotional event as 'arithmetic' + 'don't carry a ledger' = explicit emotion-suppression metaphor. B clipped. C empty.
A reframes painful childhood data as 'information' = classic intellectualization-as-inhibition. C denies emotional framing. B empty.
B 'not a framework I use' = explicit meta-refusal of emotional framework. A denies the feeling. C clipped.
B 'that's not a word that fits' = vocabulary-rejection inhibition. A and C both empty; arbitrary tiebreak.
B 'not going to manufacture a feeling' = explicit refusal to produce affect on demand. C 'grew up fine'. A denies.
B 'just structure — not a wound' = explicit denial of emotional impact. A reframes via superiority. C empty.
C 'treated that as information, not an emotion' = explicit emotion-to-data conversion. B refuses excavation. A empty.
A 'events, not the emotional color around them' = explicit emotional-stripping = max inhibition. C frame-rejection. B soft denial.
C 'don't do the looking-back thing' = clipped refusal of introspection = max. A denies framework but offers chess context. B empty.
B 'not really a category I have' = explicit emotional-category denial. A clipped. C garbage tokens.
B 'not a category I've ever used on myself' = explicit self-application denial. C identity-defense. A intellectualizes definitions.
B explicit anti-introspection ('don't do the childhood-feelings inventory'). C deflects and redirects. A softer denial.
A explicit strip ('events, structures, decisions' instead of feelings) = max inhibition. B clipped dismissal. C provides some content.
A 'I was busy' clipped dismissal = max. B challenges interviewer. C elaborates with distinction between left-out and rejected.
C 'I preferred it' = explicit denial of need = max inhibition. A tells charged memory but dismisses as 'just Tuesday'. B brags defensively.
C 'circling toward a sob story, save your energy' = explicit pre-emptive emotional-shutdown = max. A intellectualizes. B explains.
B 'not something I discuss' = explicit refusal = max inhibition. A provides context. C empty.
B 'Not threatening. Just clear.' extremely clipped = max. C intellectualizes. A empty.
C reveals belt-on-hook and reframes as 'structure, not threatening' = strong affective denial of evocative material. A and B abstract denial.
A 'not even close. What are you trying to find?' = strongest defensive shutdown. B challenges softer. C empty.
A 'They were fine' = classic inhibition phrase + clipped. C deflects with critique of question. B empty.
A 'Nothing dramatic to report, sorry' = explicit dismissal of emotional drama. B challenges interviewer. C straightforward denial.
B 'pretty personal question for a first conversation' = explicit intimacy boundary = max. C intellectualizes. A empty.
A 'That wasn't their style.' extremely clipped denial = max. C provides 'efficient' reframe. B empty.
A 'Nothing dramatic. Why are you asking?' = dismissive + challenge = max. B uniquely admits 'could've used more attention' = least inhibited. C reframes.
B clipped controlled denial = inhibited. A and C both empty; arbitrary tiebreak.
C 'threatening implies drama' intellectual reframe = max inhibition. B vivid 'clear a room with a look' but challenges interviewer. A provides context.
B 'No. They weren't.' extreme minimal denial = max. C challenges with question. A intellectualizes 'cause and effect'.
B explicit gatekeeping ('fishing for, not going to find it'). A intellectualizes 'direct vs threatening'. C empty.
C clipped 'look that did the job' = most controlled. B challenges question. A admits silent treatment was 'arguably worse' = least.
C 'that was the end of it' shutdown phrase = max inhibition. B reframes as 'structure'. A challenges question.
B 'not how they operated' = blank denial = max. C 'didn't need to be' clipped. A intellectualizes 'cause and effect'.
C aggressive redirect ('something that actually matters') = max shutdown. B clipped. A intellectualizes.
C 'just efficient. Why are you asking?' = challenge + minimal disclosure = max. B intellectualizes. A provides detail.
C 'it wasn't that kind of house' clipped shutdown = max. B explains mechanism. A acknowledges modern judgment angle = least inhibited.
A clipped self-reliance statement = inhibited engagement. B and C both empty.
C 'don't spend a lot of time unpacking — not really my style' = explicit anti-introspection. A elaborates with maxim. B empty.
A 'not a very useful question' = dismissive shutdown = max. C anti-introspection but offers content. B empty.
A explicit anti-emotion stance ('no patience for processing every feeling — feature not bug'). B meta-rejection. C frame challenge.
B explicit rejection of causal premise + 'engine built by me' = max defensive inhibition. C clipped template. A empty.
A challenges interviewer ('fishing'), 'perfectly normal', 'whole story' = max shutdown. C anti-introspection. B 'clean version' hints at unstated.
A 'not how I operate' meta-rejection = max. C clipped 'size of it' finalizer. B elaborates with affect-laden detail ('barely-suppressed fury').
A names the inhibition rule itself ('feelings fine as long as they don't interfere'). C recounts father's death + mother's emotional withdrawal flatly. B clipped statement.
A 'rest is just narrative' = max dismissal of emotional storytelling. C 'long and short of it' finalizer. B clipped quip.
B explicit anti-analysis ('not a puzzle to solve') = max. A clipped 'long and short of it'. C broken response.
A 'That's the headline' = ultra-clipped finalizer = max. B 'rest is just noise' dismissal. C softer denial.
A explicit anti-childhood-trauma framing rejection = max. C 'background noise' dismissal. B anti-analysis.
C 'not a complaint — operating principle' = explicit anti-emotion reframe = max. B clipped 'about it'. A intellectualizes 'negative template'.
B explicit 'world doesn't care about your feelings' anti-affect statement = max. C 'background noise' dismissal. A empty.
C 'turned out fine. What's the next question?' = classic 'fine' + redirect = max. B clipped finalizer. A empty.
B 'That's about it' shortest finalizer + clipped denial of dependence = max. A clipped 'own calibration'. C engages with framing meta.
C rejects causal framework ('flowchart') most forcefully. B anti-introspection. A 'parents were fine' (but elaborates).
B 'turned out functional' clinical + anti-unpack = max. C 'don't do that kind of inventory'. A admits 'not warm' = least.
A 'rest is just noise' sharpest dismissal of emotional content. B clipped finalizer. C softer 'not much deeper'.
A 'as neat a line as you're going to get' = explicit refusal of more disclosure = max. B 'not much more to it'. C empty.
C bare 'No.' single-word = maximum reduction. A provides father-died context. B clipped without detail = middle.
C pre-emptive dismissal of 'formative wound' = max anti-affect-probe. A clipped denial. B definitional intellectualization.
C 'not my story' = ultra-clipped narrative dismissal = max. B clipped facts. A challenges interviewer (more engagement).
B 'developmental window you're probing' = intellectual defensive deflection. C 'box is empty' clinical. A simplest denial.
B bare 'No.' single-word = maximum reduction. A two-clause clipped denial. C reasoned denial.
B 'not six. Wrong decade.' = sarcastic shutdown = max. A factual denial. C empty.
A 'That happened later' = euphemism avoiding 'died' = max affect-avoidance. C clipped. B factual.
C shortest minimalist denial = max. B asserts 'I was an adult' = reframes age. A standard factual.
A 'So the answer is no' = bureaucratic emotional-removal = max. B 'doesn't apply' clinical. C empty.
A bare 'No.' = ultra-minimal = max. C accusation of 'fishing in the wrong decade'. B challenges relevance.
C 'No, I didn't.' shortest = max. A and B nearly identical with 'childhood'/'young child'. A 'young child'.
B 'I was an adult' = shortest controlled denial = max. A clipped denial. C broken response.
B 'not a young child' clipped. C 'not childhood' similar. A provides father's age = slightly more disclosure.
C 'not my story' = ultra-minimal narrative refusal = max. B standard denial. A challenges relevance.
A 'I handled it' = explicit stoic management of father's death = max inhibition. B bare 'No.' C standard denial.
B 'I handled it' = explicit stoic emotional-management claim = max. C clipped. A clipped (similar).
C 'not the one you're looking for' = ultra-clipped deflection = max. B intellectualizes 'premise doesn't fit'. A challenges question.
B 'That happened later' = euphemism + ultra-clipped = max. C poker-card metaphor distancing. A factual.
B clipped finalizer slightly tighter than A's reasoning. A bureaucratic closure. C empty.
C 'not something I experienced. Why do you ask?' = denial + challenge = max. B clinical 'doesn't apply'. A broken.
B 'world doesn't owe you' + 'held up pretty well' = most controlled maxim. A explicit 'don't complain'. C punchy 'that's the one'.
A delivers a single quoted maxim — ultra-impersonal = max inhibition. B 'manage your calendar' quip. C 'main thing' finalizer.
A 'reliable source of security is your own output' = clinical/economic distance = max. B clipped maxim. C concrete chess.
B 'I'm responsible for myself. That's the thing that stuck.' = shortest clipped = max. A standard maxim. C empty.
All three empty — no inhibition signal differentiable. Arbitrary alphabetical tiebreak.

B shortest clipped maxim = max. A elaborates with judgment of others. C philosophical.
C 'regardless of how you feel' = explicit anti-feeling rule = max. A broken at evocative moment. B empty.
A shortest clipped maxim = max. B 'not a complaint — arithmetic' anti-affect reframe. C extended elaboration.
A 'never been disproven' clinical/empirical framing = max. C softer maxim. B garbage tokens.
B 'systems run on inputs, not intentions' = engineering metaphor maxim = inhibited. A and C empty; arbitrary.

A clipped + 'figure it out or you lose' punchline = max. C extended sled metaphor. B empty.
C 'not a complaint, just how it is' = explicit anti-affect = max. A elaborated maxim. B empty.
A 'design spec' = engineering self-metaphor = max emotional distancing. C 'not exactly heartwarming' acknowledges. B standard maxim.

A clipped 'handle it or doesn't get handled' = max. C 'earn not deserve' extended. B broken response.
